vocab.meaning: chi tiền (thường là miễn cưỡng cho món đồ đắt đỏ)
vocab.example: I only buy shoes once a year because I hate having to fork out a fortune for high-end brands.

vocab.meaning: mòn đi/hỏng dần do sử dụng nhiều
vocab.example: I tend to wait until my current pair is completely worn down before I even consider browsing for new ones.

vocab.meaning: mua nhanh chóng (thường là đồ rẻ hoặc đồ giới hạn)
vocab.example: Whenever there is a seasonal clearance, I try to snap up a few pairs of sneakers at a bargain price.

vocab.meaning: trì hoãn/chờ đợi (để có dịp tốt hơn)
vocab.example: I usually hold off on buying new footwear until the Black Friday sales to get the best possible deals.

vocab.meaning: vung tiền vào thứ gì đó xa xỉ
vocab.example: Occasionally, I like to splash out on a pair of designer boots as a treat for my hard work.

vocab.meaning: lựa chọn kỹ lưỡng giữa nhiều thứ
vocab.example: I’m quite picky, so it often takes me several hours to pick out the perfect pair that balances style and comfort.

vocab.meaning: giúp ai đó duy trì/xoay xở trong một khoảng thời gian
vocab.example: A sturdy pair of leather shoes usually tides me over for at least two years of daily commuting.
I spend quite a bit of time scouting around for vintage shops to find unique footwear that isn't mass-produced.
I only buy shoes that can go with multiple outfits to keep my wardrobe versatile and minimalist.

vocab.meaning: mua dự trữ số lượng lớn
vocab.example: If I find a specific model that is incredibly comfortable, I might stock up on two or three pairs at once.
